paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: The paper reports 591 high-velocity star candidates, of which 91 HVS and 107 HRS are treated as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. From the printed tables of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Tab:basic, Tab:kinematic, Tab:unbound), 11 are classified as HRS (hyper-runaway stars, P_ub > 0.5) and are included in this extraction. The remaining candidate data reside in an external CSV not available in the provided archive; consequently the full roster of HVS and HRS candidates cannot be extracted. Only these 11 confirmed unbound candidates from the 20 fastest are listed.

method_chain.summary: LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectra (R~1800, 3690-9100 Å, ~10.6 million spectra) providing radial velocities and atmospheric parameters; Gaia DR2 astrometry (positions, proper motions, parallaxes) and photometry. This step describes the raw survey data; specific quantities are extracted by the dedicated astrometric, photometric, and stellar-parameter steps below.

source_refs.context: LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2 data description

method_chain.summary: Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT with a 5 arcsec radius, producing the LAMOST-Gaia catalogue of over 10 million entries.
Select stars with positive parallax (pi > 0) and fractional parallax error f_pi = sigma_pi / pi <= 0.2 (low-f sample), yielding over 8.48 million entries.

Adopt solar Galactocentric distance R0 = 8.2 kpc, solar height z0 = 25 pc above the plane, local standard of rest circular velocity v_circ = 238 km/s, and solar peculiar motion (U, V, W) = (14.0, 12.24, 7.25) km/s.

source_refs.context: Assumptions for distance and velocity calculation

method_chain.summary: Adopt radial velocities from the LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline (RV_LASP) and apply a zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s based on cross-match with the Huang+2018 radial velocity standard star catalog (2125 common stars).

Compute Galactocentric distance r_GC and total velocity V_GC for each low-f star via Monte Carlo with 1000 realizations. For each realization, sample astrometry from a multivariate Gaussian using Gaia correlation coefficients and sample LASP radial velocity (zero-point corrected) from a Gaussian. Use the median (50th percentile) as value and 16th/84th percentiles as lower/upper uncertainties.
Use seven Galactic potential models: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, and Watkins+2019, to compute escape velocities at each star's position.

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019
parameters.value: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019

method_chain.summary: Compute the escape velocity V_esc at each star's Galactocentric distance under each of the seven Galactic potential models.

For each HiVelSC, determine the unbound probability P_ub as the fraction of the 1000 MC realizations where V_GC exceeds the escape velocity from a given potential model. Probabilities are computed for all seven models.

source_refs.context: Definition of P_ub and listing of numbers

method_chain.summary: Perform numerical orbit integration with the python package Galpy, using the MWPotential2014 potential. For each star, 1000 MC realizations of astrometry and RV are traced back 10 Gyr with a fixed time-step of 10 Myr. Record orbital eccentricity, maximum height Zmax, minimum crossing radius r_min, and orbital energy E-Phi(inf).

Compute the probability of ejection from the Galactic Center (P_gc) as the fraction of orbits with r_min < 1 kpc, and the probability of ejection from the Galactic disk (P_MW) as the fraction with r_min < 25 kpc.

Classify the 591 HiVelSCs into four types using the thresholds in Table 4: HVS (P_gc > 0.16), HRS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub > 0.5), RS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub < 0.5), and OUT (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW < 0.5). This yields 91 HVS, 107 HRS, 221 RS, 172 OUT.

Mark stars with conservative astrometric quality (astrometric_flag = 1) using criteria from Lindegren+2018 and Marchetti+2019. Additionally apply stringent parallax precision (f_pi <= 0.1) and velocity precision (sigma_V_GC / V_GC <= 0.2) to define subsamples of reliable candidates, including the 92 conservative HiVelSCs.
Determine stellar atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) from LASP and from the data-driven Payne method (DD-Payne). DD-Payne also provides [alpha/Fe].

source_refs.context: Description of atmospheric parameters and DD-Payne

method_chain.summary: Extract Gaia DR2 astrometric quantities (RA, Dec, proper motions, parallax) for the cross-matched stars. These are the observed catalogue values used as input for distance and velocity calculations.
Extract Gaia DR2 photometric quantities (G, G_BP, G_RP magnitudes) for the cross-matched stars.

source_refs.context: Table 1: Basic parameters for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel1 is classified as HRS (hyper-runaway star) with P_ub(W) = 0.996 > 0.5, indicating it is likely unbound from the Galaxy.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All relevant measurements and classification are directly available in the printed tables of the paper.

source_refs.context: Table A2: Hivel1 HRS classification with P_ub=0.996
Classification criteria and counts for HRS

Gaia DR2 astrometric reference frame is ICRS at J2015.5

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel17 is classified as a hyper-runaway star candidate (HRS) with unbound probability > 50% under all seven Galactic potential models, indicating it is likely unbound from the Galaxy.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Classification explicitly given in Table A2 as HRS, with Pub values from 0.659 to 0.953, well above 50%.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The full sample of 591 high-velocity star candidates selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2, with total velocities >445 km/s and/or unbound in at least one potential model.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only the HVS (91) and HRS (107) subclasses are considered possibly unbound from the Galaxy according to the paper's classification. The RS (221) and OUT (172) subclasses are either bound (RS) or fast halo stars not claimed unbound (OUT). The complete list of HVS and HRS candidates is stored in an external CSV file (http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv) not available in the provided archive, preventing extraction of all identifiers for these classes.

source_refs.context: Abstract stating 591 HiVelSCs and catalog URL
Classification counts (91 HVS, 107 HRS, 221 RS, 172 OUT)
Selection summary: 591 candidates defined by escaping in at least one potential model or V_GC>450 km/s

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 20 fastest HiVelSCs for which detailed measurements (positions, velocities, escape velocities, unbound probabilities, atmospheric parameters) are printed in Tables 1, 2, 6, 7, and 8.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Among these 20, 11 are classified as HRS (hyper-runaway stars, P_ub > 0.5) and therefore are possibly unbound from the Galaxy. The remaining 9 are OUT (fast halo stars) and are not considered unbound. The 11 HRS candidates are included in this extraction.

source_refs.context: The 20 fastest HiVelSCs listed in Tables 1 and 2
Table A2: Unbound probabilities and origin classes for the 20 fastest
